title: [FIRRTL] Dedup: accidentally processes modules before referenced classes

features.name: firrtl wire with class type reference
features.description: Code should include a FIRRTL wire whose type is a class reference (firrtl.class), so the wire is typed as an object handle to a named class

features.name: two private firrtl modules with wires referencing distinct public classes
features.description: Code should include two structurally identical private FIRRTL modules that each contain a wire typed as a distinct public class, making the modules appear hash-equal while actually referencing different non-deduplicable classes

features.name: firrtl circuit triggering dedup across modules with distinct public class wire types
features.description: Code should include a FIRRTL circuit where a top-level module instantiates two private modules whose only structural difference is a wire typed as distinct public classes, so the firrtl-dedup pass processes the parent modules before their class references are resolved in the dedup map
